project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS alankarashastra voice register corpus
designPillars: 11 alankarashastra voice registers (Dandin Kavyadarsha + Bhamaha Kavyalankara)
55 character→voice bindings (full ELGODS CHARACTER_VOICE_MAP + Bala Kanda additions)
36 Bala Kanda voice cues (one per Day 12 moment, mapped via protagonist→register)
Kid-friendly verbal texture descriptions (no graphic violence)
audioFile slot reserved for Day 14+ voice pipeline (currently null)

registers.description: Balanced, even syllables — the speech of kings
Rhythmic, with refrains — the speech of devotees
Cites scripture — the speech of scholars and kings of lore
Supplicating the divine — the speech of devotees
Following another's lead — the speech of devoted servants
World-weary, mournful — the speech of those who have given up
Gentle, blessing — the speech of mothers
Warning against — the speech of elder counselors
Humble, seeking help — the speech of the suffering
Principled counsel — the speech of ministers
Neutral exposition — the voice of the storyteller

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: loyal, following — like a younger sibling who always walks beside
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Lakshmana. Born of Sumitra, twin to Shatrughna, half-brother to Rama and Bharata.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: world-weary, slow — like a soul who has seen much sorrow
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Bharata. Son of Kaikeyi, the warrior-queen who saved my father's life in challenges.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: world-weary, slow — like a soul who has seen much sorrow
voiceCues.narrationLine: When I was a child, my mother Kaikeyi saved King Dasharatha in challenges — her chariot was drawn by eight wild horses, and the wheels broke on uneven ground. She fought the troublesome spirits with her bare hands.

voiceCues.speaker: kaikeyi
voiceCues.texture: humble, pleading — like a friend asking for help
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Kaikeyi. Daughter of the king of Kekaya, the warrior-kingdom in the northwest.

voiceCues.speaker: kaikeyi
voiceCues.texture: humble, pleading — like a friend asking for help
voiceCues.narrationLine: The troublesome spirits attacked at dawn. I was in my chariot.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Vishwamitra, son of the kshatriya king of Kanyakubja, born in the dynasty of Kusha. I am a king.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: I ate only forest roots. I drank only rainwater.

voiceCues.speaker: vasishtha
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Kaushika, you have done great tapas. But a brahmarshi is not made by tapas alone.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: A king of the Ikshvaku dynasty, Trishanku, asked me to perform a sacrifice to send him to heaven in his own body. The rishis at the sacrifice refused to chant.

voiceCues.speaker: menaka
voiceCues.texture: soft, worshipful — like a prayer whispered to the divine
voiceCues.narrationLine: Great sage, I was sent by Indra to distract you. I have spent a year with you, in your ashram, as your companion.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: King Dasharatha. I have come to your court to ask for your eldest son, Rama.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama. This is your first challenges.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama. The other rakshasis of the forest have come to avenge Tadaka.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama. Lakshmana.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: The thousand-year tapas is complete. The gods gather.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama, the kings of Mithila are gathering. Janaka, the great king, has declared: whoever can string the bow of Shiva — the Pinaka — will marry his daughter, Sita.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama lifts the bow. He draws it.

voiceCues.speaker: agastya
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: The day Rama was born — under the star Punarvasu, in the month of Chaitra, on the ninth day — I was the first to see it. From the southern peak of the Sahyadri mountains, I saw the sky light up.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: This is my vina. The strings are made of the breath of the wind.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am the keeper of shruti. Shruti is what is heard.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Brahma, I have come to report. I have traveled the three worlds.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: King Dasharatha. I am Narada, the devarishi.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Dasharatha's court. The great hall of the kings of Kosala.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama. I am Narada.

voiceCues.speaker: narada
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Brahma. The play has begun.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Once there was a highwayman named Ratnakara. He lived in the forest.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Brahma appeared before me. He said: "Ratnakara, you are a highwayman.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am no longer Ratnakara. I am no longer the highwayman.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am sitting by the Tamasa river. I have finished my tapas.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: Narada has come to my ashram. Narada — the devarishi — has come to the valmika.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am meditating. I am doing tapas.

voiceCues.speaker: valmiki
voiceCues.texture: neutral, even — like the storyteller reading from a book
voiceCues.narrationLine: My ashram is on the bank of the Tamasa river. The river is a tributary of the Ganga.

voiceCues.speaker: brahma
voiceCues.texture: thoughtful, citing wisdom — like a teacher explaining a riddle
voiceCues.narrationLine: Valmiki. I am Brahma.

voiceCues.speaker: urmila
voiceCues.texture: world-weary, slow — like a soul who has seen much sorrow
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Urmila. I am the daughter of King Janaka.

voiceCues.speaker: urmila
voiceCues.texture: world-weary, slow — like a soul who has seen much sorrow
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am the wife of Lakshmana. I am the sister of Sita.

voiceCues.speaker: mandavi
voiceCues.texture: measured, principled — like a wise advisor in a council
voiceCues.narrationLine: I am Mandavi. I am the daughter of King Kushadhwaja — Janaka's younger brother.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana leave Ayodhya in a simple chariot. The people weep as
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana leave Ayodhya in a simple chariot. The people weep as they follow to the river. Guha the boatman ferries them across. The forest of Dandaka awaits — wild, dangerous, and free.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: The demoness Tataka attacks in the dense forest. She was once beautiful, cursed
voiceCues.narrationLine: The demoness Tataka attacks in the dense forest. She was once beautiful, cursed by a sage to become a monster. Rama hesitates — killing a woman goes against his nature. Vishwamitra commands: Dharma sometimes requires difficult choices.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: A golden deer appears in the clearing. Sita asks Rama to catch it. Lakshmana war
voiceCues.narrationLine: A golden deer appears in the clearing. Sita asks Rama to catch it. Lakshmana warns: it is a demon in disguise. Rama follows. The deer lures him far away. Then Sita hears Rama calling: It is not his voice.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: A cry: Lakshmana, help! Sita sends Lakshmana to rescue Rama. Before he leaves, h
voiceCues.narrationLine: A cry: Lakshmana, help! Sita sends Lakshmana to rescue Rama. Before he leaves, he draws a line around the hut. Do not cross this line. Do not let anyone in. The Rakshasa cannot enter.

voiceCues.speaker: ravana
voiceCues.texture: A sage appears at the door. Ravana in disguise. Sita crosses the line she promis
voiceCues.narrationLine: A sage appears at the door. Ravana in disguise. Sita crosses the line she promised not to cross. He reveals himself — the king of Lanka. The ten-headed rakshasa lifts the earth and carries her south across the ocean.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Jatayu the eagle fought Ravana to free Sita. His wings were cut. Rama finds him
voiceCues.narrationLine: Jatayu the eagle fought Ravana to free Sita. His wings were cut. Rama finds him dying in the grass. The old bird whispers: South. Lanka. He took her. Rama holds him as he breathes his last and promises a fiery funeral.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Shabari waits in the ashram. She is old, she is tribal, she is a woman who waite
voiceCues.narrationLine: Shabari waits in the ashram. She is old, she is tribal, she is a woman who waited for Rama long before he arrived. She offers him berries — each one tasted to ensure sweetness. Rama eats them. Her devotion is sweeter than the fruit.

voiceCues.speaker: shabari
voiceCues.texture: Shabari bows. Rama lifts her. You have waited long, Mother. You have no feet to
voiceCues.narrationLine: Shabari bows. Rama lifts her. You have waited long, Mother. You have no feet to wash, but I am the one who should wash yours. She smiles. I have seen you in my heart for a hundred years.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: Bharata marches barefoot to the forest. Not to fight. Not to punish. To bring Ra
voiceCues.narrationLine: Bharata marches barefoot to the forest. Not to fight. Not to punish. To bring Rama home. He carries his brother's sandals on his head. The army follows in silence. The forest hears them coming.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: Sita is gone. Lakshmana falls to his knees. Rama lifts him: This is not your fau
voiceCues.narrationLine: Sita is gone. Lakshmana falls to his knees. Rama lifts him: This is not your fault. We will find her. Together they follow the trail of torn fabric and broken garland into the unknown south.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: In the great city of Ayodhya, King Dasharatha performed the putra-kameshti yajna
voiceCues.narrationLine: In the great city of Ayodhya, King Dasharatha performed the putra-kameshti yajna. From the sacred flames, divine payasam emerged. His queen Kausalya received it first, and on the ninth day of Chaitra, under the star Punarvasu, Rama was born.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama and his three brothers grow up together in the palace courtyard — learning
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama and his three brothers grow up together in the palace courtyard — learning archery from Vasishtha, wrestling in the gardens, and hearing stories of the gods.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: Vishwamitra asks for Rama's help. The demons Maricha and Subahu are disrupting h
voiceCues.narrationLine: Vishwamitra asks for Rama's help. The demons Maricha and Subahu are disrupting his yajna in the forest. Rama must accompany the sage with Lakshmana.

voiceCues.speaker: dasharatha
voiceCues.texture: Dasharatha tells Rama Kaikeyi's demands: fourteen years of forest exile. The kin
voiceCues.narrationLine: Dasharatha tells Rama Kaikeyi's demands: fourteen years of forest exile. The king is bound by his own promise.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: Sita refuses to stay behind. Where Rama goes, she goes. The forest queen chooses
voiceCues.narrationLine: Sita refuses to stay behind. Where Rama goes, she goes. The forest queen chooses the forest without hesitation.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: Lakshmana pledges to serve Rama in the forest. He cannot let his brother walk th
voiceCues.narrationLine: Lakshmana pledges to serve Rama in the forest. He cannot let his brother walk the path of exile alone.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: Bharata returns to Ayodhya and learns of his father's death and Rama's exile. He
voiceCues.narrationLine: Bharata returns to Ayodhya and learns of his father's death and Rama's exile. He rejects the throne Kaikeyi sought for him.

voiceCues.speaker: kaikeyi
voiceCues.texture: Kaikeyi demands the two boons Dasharatha promised her: Bharat's coronation and R
voiceCues.narrationLine: Kaikeyi demands the two boons Dasharatha promised her: Bharat's coronation and Rama's exile. Palace dharma turns to palace grief.

voiceCues.speaker: urmila
voiceCues.texture: Urmila stays in Ayodhya when Lakshmana walks into exile. She keeps the home fire
voiceCues.narrationLine: Urmila stays in Ayodhya when Lakshmana walks into exile. She keeps the home fires burning for fourteen years.

voiceCues.speaker: guha
voiceCues.texture: Guha the Nishada chief ferries Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana across the Ganga at Sri
voiceCues.narrationLine: Guha the Nishada chief ferries Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana across the Ganga at Sringaverapura. The river marks the boundary between Ayodhya and the forest.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: Bharata places Rama's sandals on the throne and rules as regent. He waits in Nan
voiceCues.narrationLine: Bharata places Rama's sandals on the throne and rules as regent. He waits in Nandigrama for the fourteen years to end.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: Lakshmana is born of Sumitra as the twin of Shatrughna. He shares a portion of V
voiceCues.narrationLine: Lakshmana is born of Sumitra as the twin of Shatrughna. He shares a portion of Vishnu's essence with Rama.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: When I see Rama, something breaks open inside me. I am Hanuman, son of Vayu. I a
voiceCues.narrationLine: When I see Rama, something breaks open inside me. I am Hanuman, son of Vayu. I am yours. I leap from the tree and land at his feet. Sugriva watches. The future of Lanka trembles.

voiceCues.speaker: jambavan
voiceCues.texture: Hanuman, remember your powers! You are the son of Vayu, the wind god. You are th
voiceCues.narrationLine: Hanuman, remember your powers! You are the son of Vayu, the wind god. You are the eleventh avatar of Rudra. Leap, Vanara. The ocean cannot hold you. Lanka cannot hide from you.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: Hanuman grows to the size of a mountain. He crouches on the mountain peak and la
voiceCues.narrationLine: Hanuman grows to the size of a mountain. He crouches on the mountain peak and launches himself across the ocean. The waves bow. The fish look up. Lanka sees his shadow and trembles.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: The Ashoka grove. I climb a flowering tree and look down. Sita sits in the cente
voiceCues.narrationLine: The Ashoka grove. I climb a flowering tree and look down. Sita sits in the center, surrounded by rakshasas. Her hair is unbound. Her face is a moon behind clouds. I drop a ring. She looks up.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: I find her under the Ashoka tree. Mother Sita, Rama sent me. I am his messenger.
voiceCues.narrationLine: I find her under the Ashoka tree. Mother Sita, Rama sent me. I am his messenger. She does not believe — until I give her the ring. The ring he gave her at their wedding. Recognition breaks like dawn.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: Rama is alive. He is coming. He sent this ring as proof. But will he come? Or ha
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama is alive. He is coming. He sent this ring as proof. But will he come? Or has fourteen years broken his love? Hanuman bows. I will carry you on my back if I have to. She laughs — the first laugh in Lanka.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: They set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop, setting Lanka ablaz
voiceCues.narrationLine: They set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop, setting Lanka ablaze. Not out of rage — out of necessity. The city of Lanka teaches respect for one vanara's tail.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: Lakshmana falls in the war. I fly to the Himalayas and lift the entire mountain
voiceCues.narrationLine: Lakshmana falls in the war. I fly to the Himalayas and lift the entire mountain — the mountain of herbs — and carry it back. The rishi is surprised. The mountain is surprised. Lanka sees the shadow of a flying peak.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama meets Sugriva in the Rishyamooka hills. Two exiles. Two crowns lost. One pr
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama meets Sugriva in the Rishyamooka hills. Two exiles. Two crowns lost. One promise made: I will help you regain your kingdom; you will help me find Sita. The alliance is sealed not by oath but by fire.

voiceCues.speaker: sugriva
voiceCues.texture: Sugriva is crowned king of Kishkindha. Rama stands beside him. The vanara army i
voiceCues.narrationLine: Sugriva is crowned king of Kishkindha. Rama stands beside him. The vanara army is assembled. The southern ocean waits. Hanuman waits. The road to Lanka is open.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama returns to Ayodhya. The city is illuminated with a million lamps — it is th
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama returns to Ayodhya. The city is illuminated with a million lamps — it is the first Deepavali. Bharata rushes to his brother's feet. The throne was always yours. I kept it safe. The people weep with joy.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama is crowned king of Ayodhya. Bharata places the crown upon his head. Sita st
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama is crowned king of Ayodhya. Bharata places the crown upon his head. Sita stands beside him. The city is lit with a million lamps. The fourteen years are over. Ram Rajya begins — an age of justice, prosperity, and peace.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Ram Rajya begins. No hunger. No fear. No one goes to bed hungry. No one dies of
voiceCues.narrationLine: Ram Rajya begins. No hunger. No fear. No one goes to bed hungry. No one dies of disease. The cows give milk. The trees give fruit. The rivers give water. Ayodhya becomes the city the world envies.

voiceCues.speaker: urmila
voiceCues.texture: Urmila and Lakshmana reunite after fourteen years. She kept the home fires burni
voiceCues.narrationLine: Urmila and Lakshmana reunite after fourteen years. She kept the home fires burning. He kept the war alive. Two sacrifices, two paths, one household. The palace courtyard is full again.

voiceCues.speaker: guha
voiceCues.texture: Rama returns to the banks of the Ganga. Guha the Nishada chief waits. The ferrym
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama returns to the banks of the Ganga. Guha the Nishada chief waits. The ferryman who carried the exiles now greets the king. The river remembers. The forest remembers. The friendship outlasted the exile.

voiceCues.speaker: kausalya
voiceCues.texture: Kausalya waits in the palace. Her son returns. She does not ask about the war. S
voiceCues.narrationLine: Kausalya waits in the palace. Her son returns. She does not ask about the war. She does not ask about Lanka. She touches his face and blesses him. A mother's blessing is the oldest shield in the world.

voiceCues.speaker: vishwamitra
voiceCues.texture: Vishwamitra watches from the eastern gate. The boy who came to my ashram is now
voiceCues.narrationLine: Vishwamitra watches from the eastern gate. The boy who came to my ashram is now king. The demoness Tataka fell to his first arrow. Now he rules the kingdom he saved. The rishi smiles. This is the fruit of tapas.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama performs the Ashvamedha yajna. The horse is released. The kingdom is consec
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama performs the Ashvamedha yajna. The horse is released. The kingdom is consecrated. Sita watches from a distance — she who cannot enter the hall because of the kingdom's silence. The sacrifice is complete. The kingdom is whole.

voiceCues.speaker: lava
voiceCues.texture: Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana outside the royal hall. They are twins born in
voiceCues.narrationLine: Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana outside the royal hall. They are twins born in the forest, raised by Valmiki. Rama weeps. Sita watches from the balcony. The song ends. She descends into the earth that bore her.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Time to leave. Not death — departure. Rama walks into the Sarayu river. The wate
voiceCues.narrationLine: Time to leave. Not death — departure. Rama walks into the Sarayu river. The water does not wet him. The kingdom watches. Bharata runs after him. Lakshmana runs. The rope they lay behind them disappears. He is not gone. He is everywhere.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama meets Hanuman in the Rishyamooka hills. The vanara is wild, loud, and loyal
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama meets Hanuman in the Rishyamooka hills. The vanara is wild, loud, and loyal. When Hanuman sees Rama, something breaks open inside him. I am yours. The alliance that will move mountains is sealed in one glance.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: Hanuman grows to mountain size. He crouches on Mainaka and launches himself acro
voiceCues.narrationLine: Hanuman grows to mountain size. He crouches on Mainaka and launches himself across the southern ocean. Surasa opens her mouth to swallow him; he shrinks and enters, then expands and escapes. Sinhika tries to grab his shadow; he cuts her in half. The waves bow. Lanka sees his shadow and trembles.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: The war is over. Rama stands before Sita in the field of Lanka. His eyes are col
voiceCues.narrationLine: The war is over. Rama stands before Sita in the field of Lanka. His eyes are cold. Let Agni, the fire, be the witness of your purity. Sita walks into the flames. The fire does not burn her. The gods witness: she is untouched.

voiceCues.speaker: trijata
voiceCues.texture: Trijata, the rakshasi who dreams true, visits Sita in the Ashoka grove. I have s
voiceCues.narrationLine: Trijata, the rakshasi who dreams true, visits Sita in the Ashoka grove. I have seen Rama with an army of monkeys. I have seen Ravana fall. Lanka will burn. Wait, Mother. Victory is closer than tomorrow.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: The rakshasas set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop across Lank
voiceCues.narrationLine: The rakshasas set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop across Lanka. Each touch ignites a demonstration of power. By dawn, Lanka is a lesson written in flame. Not out of rage — out of necessity.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: After the fire, Sita turns to the earth — her mother, Bhumi. I call upon you. Ta
voiceCues.narrationLine: After the fire, Sita turns to the earth — her mother, Bhumi. I call upon you. Take me home. The ground splits open. A great throne rises. She descends into the earth that bore her. No argument. No doubt. She is claimed.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Ravana falls. The ten-headed tyrant who kidnapped dharma itself is defeated by t
voiceCues.narrationLine: Ravana falls. The ten-headed tyrant who kidnapped dharma itself is defeated by the arrow of dharma. Rama does not gloat. He steps over the body and walks to the Ashoka grove. The war is over. The real victory is witnessing Sita walk out alive.

voiceCues.speaker: mandodari
voiceCues.texture: Mandodari stands before Ravana in the war chamber. She pleads: return Sita. Let
voiceCues.narrationLine: Mandodari stands before Ravana in the war chamber. She pleads: return Sita. Let dharma save Lanka. He laughs: I am Ravana. She turns away. Her silence is louder than his drums. The last voice of reason dies in the palace.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama is crowned king of Ayodhya. Bharata places the crown upon his head. Sita st
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama is crowned king of Ayodhya. Bharata places the crown upon his head. Sita stands beside him. The city is lit with a million lamps. The fourteen years are over. Ram Rajya begins — an age of justice, prosperity, and peace.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: Rama offers Hanuman any reward. I ask only to remain your servant. Wherever your
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama offers Hanuman any reward. I ask only to remain your servant. Wherever your name is spoken, there I will be. Hanuman becomes the symbol of selfless service that outlives kings, cities, and ages.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: I place Rama's sandals on the throne. For 14 years, I govern in his name. The sa
voiceCues.narrationLine: I place Rama's sandals on the throne. For 14 years, I govern in his name. The sandals rule, not Bharata. Every decree is checked against the brother who is not here. The kingdom learns the meaning of trust.

voiceCues.speaker: kaikeyi
voiceCues.texture: Fourteen years alone. Fear made me the villain. Fear of losing everything. Which
voiceCues.narrationLine: Fourteen years alone. Fear made me the villain. Fear of losing everything. Which is exactly what I caused. Bharata will not forgive me. The kingdom whispers. I sit in the empty palace and wait for the return I demanded.

voiceCues.speaker: kaikeyi
voiceCues.texture: Mother! You killed father! I will never forgive you! Bharata's words echo throug
voiceCues.narrationLine: Mother! You killed father! I will never forgive you! Bharata's words echo through the palace corridors. Kaikeyi hears them every morning. She does not answer. She cannot. Some griefs have no rebuttal.

voiceCues.speaker: mandodari
voiceCues.texture: Fire. Hanuman has set Lanka alight. The women of the palace weep. Trijata tells
voiceCues.narrationLine: Fire. Hanuman has set Lanka alight. The women of the palace weep. Trijata tells me this is not destruction — it is reckoning. Mandodari stands in the ash and dismisses the last illusion: Ravana is not invincible.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: A citizen questions Sita's purity. Rama does not argue. He asks Sita to prove he
voiceCues.narrationLine: A citizen questions Sita's purity. Rama does not argue. He asks Sita to prove herself. She agrees. She walks into the fire. The fire does not burn her. The gods witness. She returns. But Rama exiles her anyway — for the kingdom's silence must be honored.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: Rama asks me to take Sita to the forest. Pregnant. Alone. This is the hardest th
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama asks me to take Sita to the forest. Pregnant. Alone. This is the hardest thing I have ever done. I take her to the ashram. I build the hut. I stay until the children are born. Then I return to Ayodhya with an empty heart.

voiceCues.speaker: shabari
voiceCues.texture: Sixty years. The ascetics laugh. Sometimes I doubt. But the waiting itself is pr
voiceCues.narrationLine: Sixty years. The ascetics laugh. Sometimes I doubt. But the waiting itself is prayer. Shabari sits in the ashram and watches the road. She has no feet to walk to Ayodhya, but her heart arrived before Bharata's sandals.

voiceCues.speaker: lava
voiceCues.texture: Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana in the court of Ayodhya. They are twins, born i
voiceCues.narrationLine: Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana in the court of Ayodhya. They are twins, born in the forest, raised by Valmiki. Rama weeps. Sita watches from the palace balcony. The song ends. She descends into the earth that bore her.

voiceCues.speaker: sita
voiceCues.texture: I call upon my mother — the Earth herself. Take me home. The ground splits open.
voiceCues.narrationLine: I call upon my mother — the Earth herself. Take me home. The ground splits open. A great throne rises. She descends. The sky darkens. Rama runs to the edge and holds out his hands. The earth does not give her back.

voiceCues.speaker: rama
voiceCues.texture: Rama weeps at the edge of the earth. He has won the war. He has won the kingdom.
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama weeps at the edge of the earth. He has won the war. He has won the kingdom. He has lost the woman who walked into fire for him. The ashram is empty. The forest is empty. The earth has taken back its daughter.

voiceCues.speaker: vibhishana
voiceCues.texture: Vibhishana reveals Ravana's secrets. Every secret is a knife in his brother's ba
voiceCues.narrationLine: Vibhishana reveals Ravana's secrets. Every secret is a knife in his brother's back. He crosses the ocean to Rama's camp. Lakshmana doubts him. Rama embraces him. Dharma chooses the righteous, not the kin.

voiceCues.speaker: hanuman
voiceCues.texture: Rama sends a ring as proof. Hanuman arrives. As a child you mistook the sun for
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama sends a ring as proof. Hanuman arrives. As a child you mistook the sun for a fruit. Indra struck you down. The gods gave you powers but curbed your pride. Now you fly. Now you remember. The tests of the past become the weapons of the war.

voiceCues.speaker: kumbhakarna
voiceCues.texture: Ravana wakes Kumbhakarna from his six-month sleep. The giant rises, shakes the p
voiceCues.narrationLine: Ravana wakes Kumbhakarna from his six-month sleep. The giant rises, shakes the palace, and asks why he was disturbed. He asks for food — thousands of buffalo gallons of honey, ghee, and meat. Then he marches to the battlefield and charges the vanara army.

voiceCues.speaker: lakshmana
voiceCues.texture: Indrajit's arrows strike Lakshmana. He falls. Hanuman carries the mountain from
voiceCues.narrationLine: Indrajit's arrows strike Lakshmana. He falls. Hanuman carries the mountain from the Himalayas. Lakshmana rises. The war resumes with equal fury. Ravana's greatest weapon is matched by Rama's most faithful brother.

voiceCues.speaker: tara
voiceCues.texture: Tara speaks into the war council. Her voice is quiet but every head turns. Sugri
voiceCues.narrationLine: Tara speaks into the war council. Her voice is quiet but every head turns. Sugriva looks at her. Vali's legacy — and Sugriva's honor — are both hers to shape. Lanka hears her counsel before it hears the drum.

voiceCues.speaker: mandodari
voiceCues.texture: Rama walks past Hanuman, past Vibhishana, past Lakshmana. He stops before Mandod
voiceCues.narrationLine: Rama walks past Hanuman, past Vibhishana, past Lakshmana. He stops before Mandodari. He bends his head. I have lost my son. But Lanka still has a queen. She stands. The war council watches. The war continues.

voiceCues.speaker: mandodari
voiceCues.texture: My son. My Meghanada. They brought his body on a golden bier. Lakshmana killed h
voiceCues.narrationLine: My son. My Meghanada. They brought his body on a golden bier. Lakshmana killed him — by a trick, a holy trick, sanctioned by the gods. She holds the body and refuses to let go until dawn.

voiceCues.speaker: ravana
voiceCues.texture: Thirteen months, Sita. Thirteen months I have asked. Be my queen. Half of Lanka
voiceCues.narrationLine: Thirteen months, Sita. Thirteen months I have asked. Be my queen. Half of Lanka is yours. Ravana visits her in the Ashoka grove one last time. She refuses without looking. The king of Lanka who moved the earth cannot move one woman's heart.

voiceCues.speaker: vibhishana
voiceCues.texture: Brother, return Sita. Lanka will burn. Vibhishana stands in Ravana's court and n
voiceCues.narrationLine: Brother, return Sita. Lanka will burn. Vibhishana stands in Ravana's court and names the omens. The rains fail. The cows give blood instead of milk. The drums sound without hands. Lanka is already burning.

voiceCues.speaker: bharata
voiceCues.texture: After 14 years, Rama returns. Bharata falls at his feet. The throne was always y
voiceCues.narrationLine: After 14 years, Rama returns. Bharata falls at his feet. The throne was always yours. I kept it safe. The crowns, the sandals, the kingdom — all returned. Ayodhya lights up with a million lamps. The exile is over.

_meta.notes: Day 13 voice register corpus. 11 canonical alankarashastra registers + 55 character bindings + 36 voice cue slots for Day 12 Bala Kanda moments. Audio content (audioFile) reserved for Day 14+ voice pipeline. Verbal texture descriptions give the voice actor / TTS engine a kid-friendly reference for how each cue should sound. | Expanded to 108 cues covering all 8 kandas.
